violations.text: The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Establishment is not to use dishwasher until it is able to reach the proper chlorine sanitizing solution. Establishment will use 3-bay sink until dishwasher is serviced. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Establishment is to label all spray bottles. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the bar. Establishment is to obtain thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. Per PIC, ice machine will be emptied and cleaned at the end of night. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

The floor in the upstairs storage area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/04/2026

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service was on-site at the end of the inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Ice used for beverages was observed in direct contact with ice used to cool beverage lines. Ice used for food must be protected from contamination. Staff placed a separation barrier during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
A bag of onions and a container of pickles were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the upstairs storage area. Inspector observed cases of single service items on the floor. Items relocated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sanitizing solution for the dish machines and 3-bay sinks. Inspector provided a small supply at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in fan guards and the ice machine air filter have an accumulation of dust and dirt. The inside of the microwave has an accumulation of food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2024
Faucet at handwash sink in the kitchen was observed in poor repair. Hot water handle does not turn off, must be turned off by supply valve. New Violation. Correct By: 07/18/2024
